sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text:
This standard applies to the animal fats described in Section 2 presented in a state for human consumption.

Colour: rendered pork fat: white when solid lard: white to cream premier jus: creamy white to pale yellow edible tallow: off white to pale yellow Odour and taste: characteristic and free from foreign and rancid odour and taste. Maximum level Matter volatile at 105 °C: 0.3% Insoluble impurities: 0.05% Sodium soap content: lard nil premier jus nil rendered pork fat 0.005% edible tallow 0.005% Iron (Fe): 1.5 mg/kg Copper (Cu): 0.4 mg/kg Acid value: lard 1.3 mg KOH/g fat = ffa max 0.65% premier jus 2.0 mg KOH/g fat = ffa max 1.00% rendered pork fat 2.5 mg KOH/g fat = ffa max 1.25% edible tallow 2.5 mg KOH/g fat = ffa max 1.25% Peroxide value: up to 10 milliequivalents active oxygen/kg fat

sections.title: Lard
sections.text: Pure rendered lard is the fat rendered from fresh, clean, sound fatty tissues from swine ( Sus scrofa) in good health, at the time of slaughter, and fit for human consumption. The tissues d o not include bones, detached skin, head skin, ears, tails, organs, windpipes, large blood vessels, scrap fat, skimmings, settlings, pressings, and the like, and are reasonably free from muscle tissues and blood. Lard subject to processing  may contain refi ned lard, lard stearin and hydrogenated lard, or be subject to processes of modification provided that it is clearly labelled.

The tissues d o not include bones, detached skin, head skin, ears, tails, organs, windpipes, large blood vessels, scrap fat, skimmings, settlings, pressings, and the like, and are reasonably free from muscle tissues and blood. Lard subject to processing  may contain refi ned lard, lard stearin and hydrogenated lard, or be subject to processes of modification provided that it is clearly labelled.

The tissues d o not include bones, detached skin, head skin, ears, tails, organs, windpipes, large blood vessels, scrap fat, skimmings, settlings, pressings, and the like, and are reasonably free from muscle tissues and blood. Lard subject to processing  may contain refi ned lard, lard stearin and hydrogenated lard, or be subject to processes of modification provided that it is clearly labelled.

sections.title: Rendered pork fat
sections.text: Rendered pork fat is the fat rendered from the tissues and bones of swine (Sus scrofa) in good health, at the time of slaughter, and fit for human consumption. It may contain fat from bones (properly cleaned), from detached skin, from head skin, from ears, from tails and from other issues fit for human consumption. Rendered pork fat subject to processing may al so contain refined lard, refined rendered pork fat, hydrogenated lard, hydrogenated rendered pork fat, lard stearin and rendered pork fat stearin provided that it is clearly labelled.

sections.title: Premier jus (oleo stock)
sections.text: Premier jus (oleo stock) is the product obtained by rendering at low heat the fresh fat (killing fat) of heart, caul, kidney and mesentery collected at the time of slaughter of bovine animals in good health at the time of slaughter and fit for human consumption, as well as cutting fats.

sections.title: Edible tallow
sections.text: Edible tallow (dripping)  is the product obtained by rendering the clean, sound, fatty tissues (including trimming and cutting fats), attendant muscles and bones of bovine animals and/or sheep ( Ovis aries) in good health at the time of slaughter and fit for human consumption. Edible tallow subject to processing may contain refined edible tallow, provided that it is clearly labelled.

Edible tallow subject to processing may contain refined edible tallow, provided that it is clearly labelled.

page_spans.text: Edible tallow (dripping)  is the product obtained by rendering the clean, sound, fatty tissues (including trimming and cutting fats), attendant muscles and bones of bovine animals and/or sheep ( Ovis aries) in good health at the time of slaughter and fit for human consumption. Edible tallow subject to processing may contain refined edible tallow, provided that it is clearly labelled.

sections.title: ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.text:
Gas-liquid chromatography (GLC) ranges of fatty acid composition (expressed as percentages) Samples falling within the appropriate ranges specified below are in compliance with this standard.

For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Antifoaming agents, antioxidants and colours used in accordance with Table 1 and Table 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 in food category 02.1.3 (Lard, tallow, fish oil, and other animal fats) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The product shall be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985). The name of the fat shall conform to the descriptions given in Section 2 of this standard.

sections.title: Labelling on non-retail containers
sections.text: Information on the above labelling requirements shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the food, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sections.title: APPENDIX
sections.text: OTHER QUALITY AND COMPOSITION FACTORS These quality and composition factors are supplementary information to the essential composition and quality factors of the standard. A product, which meets the essential quality and composition factors but does not meet these supplementary factors, may still conform to the standard.

classification.pattern: Subject + shall + conform + to + Object + reference

components.subject: The name of the fat
components.object: the descriptions given in Section 2 of this standard

classification.justification: Typical obligation sentence stating conformity requirement with 'shall conform' and reference, a common regulatory normative pattern.

sentences.original: Information on the above labelling requirements shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the food, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container.

classification.sentence: However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
classification.pattern: Subject + may be replaced + Condition Clause

components.subject: lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer
components.permission: may be replaced by an identification mark
components.condition: provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents

classification.justification: Permission modality with condition; partial match to permission templates.

sentences.original: OTHER QUALITY AND COMPOSITION FACTORS

classification.sentence: A product, which meets the essential quality and composition factors but does not meet these supplementary factors, may still conform to the standard.
classification.pattern: Subject + relative clause + but + negative clause + modal verb + main verb

components.subject: A product
components.condition: which meets the essential quality and composition factors but does not meet these supplementary factors
components.permission: may still conform to the standard

classification.justification: Sentence uses conditional and permissive modality expressing an exception to full conformity; structure is more complex than typical permissions hence adapted template.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR NAMED ANIMAL FATS
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento e sua designação, com informação de adoção e emendas. Apenas o título nível 1 identificado, sem subtítulos.
Página contém um título principal de seção '2023 Amendment' em caixa alta e seu texto explicativo. Identificado cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 211-1999 2'. Não há continuidade de seções entre páginas.

A página contém os títulos das seções 1. SCOPE e 2. DESCRIPTION com suas subseções numeradas 2.1 a 2.4. O texto está corretamente associado a cada título, sem sinais de continuidade entre páginas. O cabeçalho 'CXS 211-1999 3' foi identificado e preservado.

Esta página contém as seções 3, 4 e 5 do documento, com a seção 3 marcada como continuando na próxima página devido à tabela extensa e possível continuação. O texto interno está associado corretamente a cada título e subtítulo detectado.

Página contém as seções 6, 7 (com subseções 7.1 e 7.2) e 8 do documento, cada uma com texto interno associado corretamente. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas para essas seções nesta página.

A página apresenta três seções de nível 1: 'APPENDIX', '1 QUALITY CHARACTERISTICS' e '2 CHEMICAL AND PHYSICAL CHARACTERISTICS'. O conteúdo de cada seção é associado corretamente sem subtítulos. A seção '1 QUALITY CHARACTERISTICS' continua para a próxima página evidente pelo texto extenso e falta de fechamento claro. Não há indicações de continuidade com a página anterior.

A página contém uma seção de nível 1 numerada '3 METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING' com texto associado explicando o uso dos métodos recomendados. A seção é auto-contida nesta página, sem indicações de continuidade para ou da página anterior. Notas e referências são interpretadas como parte do conteúdo da página, não como estrutura.
